channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo… — 309 replies — 264.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo…

Open thread • Replies: 309 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:02 UTC

OP

Cerebras IPO May 14th edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/NyzbAsX4

PREVIOUS
https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/62217769/smg-stock-market-general#top

Early replies (first 3)

62218691
> futures

62218697
AVGO, SITM, DVN & GIS
WY to 30 eventually

62218700
INTC ain’t stopping until 2T in market cap. Believe it (dattebayo)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62219835
>>62219564
I'm charging it out at as a variation with a floating average for larger clients.
And an inflated fixed cost average for smaller clients.

It took a lot of negotiations initially but the reality set in to developers.
My diesel costs per month went from $30k to nearly $80k. I have staff vehicles as well as machines

62219848
>>62219835
>>62219570
for

62219849
>>62218685
Anyone buying this IPO?

I don't want to be a goyim.

62219854
I have an important question

With ibkr

can you buy fractional shares with Japanese stocks? By fractional, I mean, can you buy 1 share? It's hard for me to hit my exact balance with Japanese stocks when I have to buy 100 lot shares. I know ibkr used to require you to buy 100x lots of Japanese shares. I just wonder if they let you buy 1 shares, instead of forcing 100. Its hard to weight this shit when 100 shares of some JP companies is $50,000 lol

62219860
>>62219854
No.
The asian Stock Exchanges have minimums

62219876
>>62219860
Are you sure? Because I checked online, and some sources said yes, and others no

Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?

62219878
>>62219860
And by the way, it doesnt matter if the asian stock exchanges require minimums if the broker does fractional shares. I want to know if IBKR specifically does fractional shares for JP stocks.

62219882
>>62219854
If you're a Euro, use Trading 212

62219884
>>62219876
>Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?
How about right now?

62219889
now the market wants to dip

62219892
>>62219882
>>62219860
>>62219884
But Gemini says yes

62219894
>>62219889

62219897
>>62219892
>But Gemini says yes
Why are you asking AI?
And why are you asking us?
You can either do it or you can't, and you can't.

62219906
>>62219884
Check your settings in trading permissions. See if you have fractional shares enabled.

62219909
>>62219906
No, you.

62219914
>>62219909
Please

62219922
>>62219909
If you do it, Ill tell you what JP tickers I want to buy

62219931
>>62219922
You need an account registered as "expert trader" to unlock the option.
So if you want to try it out, you'll have to lie and claim you're an expert with over 10 years experience

62219938
>>62219931
>He isn't an expert with over 10 years experience

NGMI

62219986
>>62219931
why wont you just lie?

62220010
>>62219986
Because an investor only has so many lies in a day and he needs to save them for explaining to the wife whete all their money went.

62220032
>>62220010
thats bullshit
but I believe it

62220034
>>62219986
Because I told the truth about my experience when I opened the account. Now I have to make up some lie to my broker how I instantly gained 8 years experience to show an anonymous frog whether he can buy fractional anime shares.

62220186
why are prediction markets legal? It's literally gambling, and zero sum

62224103
why didn't anons kill the thread?

item.title: Two positive hantavirus cases in the US. — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Two positive hantavirus cases in the US.

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-05-13 04:04 UTC

OP

Two positive hantavirus cases in the US.

Early replies (first 3)

62214336
>>62214321
you live in squalor

62214342
>>62214314
>>62214321
>>62214336

If it's positive what's the big deal?

62214345
>>62214314
Just sold 100k, thanks!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214347
>(((chatgpt))) trying to downplay the real threat to goyim

62214418
>>62214314
mva-hanta vax ;)

62214439
>>62214314
Those are rookie numbers, you gotta bump those numbers up to bring down oil prices.

62214464
>>62214321
>we're veterans of losing the "don't eat rat feces while having unprotected gay sex" challenge, greetings.

62214673
>>62214314
Oh... Oh my fucking god. I just reentered the market.

62214681
>>62214673
you know india's stock market just crashed and the "trump insider" with 100% win record opened a short for tomorrow morning?

62214716
>>62214314
Its already priced in

62214727
>>62214314
hyperbullish PFE

62214862
>>62214314
there is only one confirmed case and its in a lab for studying. Nice try though. People are so retarded for selling. lol

https://www.foxnews.com/health/american-evacuated-cruise-ship-tests-positive-hantavirus-another-symptoms-hhs-confirms

62214899
>>62214314
All the fatties in the chud states about to die

62214905
>hentaivirus
what did they mean by this?

62214952
>>62214321
Some people are just born mentally ill. Being in a first world country doesn’t protect from that.

62215045
>>62214314
>he doesn't know
Hantavirus is endemic in the US, and has been as long as humans have been here. Gene Hackman's wife died of Hantavirus last year. It's a fucking nothingburger.

62215060
>>62214336
Miguel Squalor

62215111
>>62214681
American short? Pic?

62215944
>>62214314
Aint that shit extremely difficult to get?

62218252
guys a hantavirus just flew over my house

62219214
>>62214314
What is the correlation between a positive test and a supposed symptom? Do symptomatic people test positive more often than asymptomatic people? In which percentage?
Does the test have a gold standard, something that it is compared to, like it is supposed to be done, and if yes, what is it?
Is it a PCR test? If yes, did they isolate the supposed virus sequence, with a control experiment?
Is it an antigenic test? If yes, did they test the positivity being exclusively positive with an isolated virus (with control experiment), and nothing else?

62219216
>>62215944
What disease is extremely easy to get? I mean done in real conditions, with control experiment?

62219218
>>62219216
>What disease is extremely easy to get?
HIV if you're down to clown ;)

62219270
>>62214314
Pfizer and Netflix buy signal?

62223055
>>62219218
Absolutely not. Real life conditions experiments have been done for supposed sexual transmitted disease. It doesn't work. Supposed HIV "virus" has never been isolated, like any other supposed "virus". Germ theory of disease is a fraud. Contagion is a scam.

62223068
AIDS was caused by Amyl nitrite (poppers) in homosexual communities, and other drugs.

62224245
>>62219216
Ever gotten a cold sore?
Congratulations, you have herpes for life!
The symptoms may go away, but there is no cure, it's inside you forever.

62224259
>>62214314
>Another Chinkoid flu
Eh, its fine. It'll kill mostly retards, old people and browns who don't understand basic hygiene. Nothing of value will be lost.

62224279
>>62214314
I bought Hanta coin, if we all die I’ll at least die rich.

62224315
>>62224245
I remember having cold sores as a kid. Am I misremembering this or did kids get cold sores before?

62224333
>>62224315
Was it a cold sore or was it just a rash around your nose from being a about-nosed kid

item.title: Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)… — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)…

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-13 04:01 UTC

OP

Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k) and focus on finishing my masters and not huffing fumes in traffic?


I feel like i wont regret giving up wfh later in life but I'll be miserable for the next 3 years.. I feel like im fucked either way.

Early replies (first 3)

62222340
>>62222336
Meant to say later in life like 3 years later when I quit cuz I cant go in the office my while life, id rather be dead. But it might be worth it to suck it up for a few years

62222349
What's the masters in? This is the hinge the decision should be on.

62222450
>>62222336
Consider how much money you would spend on gas commuting everyday for a year, and consider that gas could still go up another 50% from today's prices. So you know that you are spending $3.5k — 5.2k in gas this year citing today's average American gas price for calculations. Is office life worth this sacrifice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222880
>>62222336
take the 50% raise wtf

62222939
how long is the gommute

62222960
please kill yourself

62223191
>>62222349
Electrical Engineering

62223303
>>62222939
The goymute is 42 miles so I would have to move or get a tesla with fsd

62223366
Things are about to get COVID style unstable,band unemployment is at 30%. I'd go with whichever job is less likely to terminate early. Any steady paycheck is better than no paycheck.

62223378
>>62223303
I personally would not want to spend 2 hrs a day commuting. That time could be spent doing sports, dating, another side hustle that I really like, etc.

62223393
>>62223303
I would not unless you are open to moving closer.
>>62223366
Also this

I would probably kms if I had to do an actual rigorous masters while working full time in office desu

62223417
>>62222880
Ikr, yet a lot of people here and a big part of myself really want work life balance but 50 percent raise should be a no brainer

62223423
>>62223378
I cant afford to date on 105k a year also im an engineer so what dates?

62223427
>>62223393
I am thinking of killing myself. I am open to moving but its in a liberal hellhole. Gotta do what I gotta do.

62224325
>>62222336
Get a job that's no easily replaced by AI
If you commute buy a Tesla with fsd so you don't have to actively drive and spend the time doing something productive.

NEW TRUMP POST

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-13 04:11 UTC

OP

Bears are fucked

Early replies (first 3)

62224296

62224311
>>62224292
>>62224296
Putin, you know what to do

62224318
>>62224311
Fucking RIP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224322
>>62224311
digits and market closes +2.4% tomorrow

62224324
>>62224296
You secure that shit anon.

62224334
>>62224296
all the for the market to contract 0.55%

62224335
>>62224311
black Wednesday it is

62224338
>>62224322
this but its bond yields

62224348
The day the market died is upon us

item.title: Australia is fucked — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Australia is fucked

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-13 04:11 UTC

OP

>CGT discount - DENIED
>Negative Gearing - DENIED
Where were you when the Australian dream died?

Early replies (first 3)

62224090
have the liberals said they will reverse it? do we just hold and wait?

62224122
>>62223850
Doesn’t this come into effect in 2027? Should I sell all my crypto before 2027?

62224148
>>62223850
its tiring living here
the new rules favour low return long duration assets. high growth assets like btc and leveraged etfs get smoked compared old rules. run a few tests... under 12 months nothing changes.
i wonder if it actually encourages speculation. might as well punt on options or asx bets miners or U.S letfs... you need BIG wins now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224204
>>62223850
Negative gearing has always been retarded.

62224281
>>62223850
Explain whats changed so i can care or not ?
t aussie

62224288
>>62223850
>the Australian dream
KEK
Australia was a prison land where we sent the scum to so they can starve or kill each other.
Only after the jews mettled did they get rights.

62224331
>>62224281
No more long term CGT discount. Minimum taxed is now always 30% even if you used to be in lower bracket

62224341
>>62224288
>where we sent
I really hope this isn't a bong posting, because lol if you think bongs are in any position to be talking like this in current year.

62224346
>>62224288
Shalom!

item.title: TRUMP FLYING TO BEIJING — 7 replies — 22.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
TRUMP FLYING TO BEIJING

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:54 UTC

OP

With dozens of CEOs and his billionaire buddies.

And you’re bearish?

Early replies (first 3)

62224017
>>62224013
One surface to air missile and the economy collapses

62224061
One surface to air missile and the economy collapses

62224077
Collapses economy the and missile air to surface one.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224080
One surface to air missile and the economy moons.

62224084
>>62224017
everyone on board is replaceable

62224087
Niggervideo is rugging in overnight there better be a deal

62224307
>>62224013
Why does NVIDIA have an eye for an logo?

Good luck faggots of Babylon.

item.title: The tragedy of Berkshire is that it assumed a level of long-term rationality in the market that the last 20 years shows… — 13 replies — 12.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
The tragedy of Berkshire is that it assumed a level of long-term rationality in the market that the last 20 years shows…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:12 UTC

OP

The tragedy of Berkshire is that it assumed a level of long-term rationality in the market that the last 20 years shows is just not there. The Berkshire model stops working when IQs fall so much that P/Es of 40x are just accepted as normal by almost everyone and memecoin pump-and-dumps are the highest-EV thing the average man can invest in.

Early replies (first 3)

62219830
>>62219808
buffet doesnt care about stock price. he buys profitable businesses

62219837
>>62219830
A stock is only so profitable relative to what you had to pay to get a share of those produts.

62219841
>>62219837
correct. so as long as he bought them at a fair price its all good

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62219845
>>62219808
Valuation ratios don't really have any relevance when we live in a world with global capital inflows to markets, as well as central bank funny money (aka money printing).

No shit stuff is gonna look expensive when you print away the currency.

62219948
oh no, whatever will they do with their hundreds of billions of dollars? it's over...

62219965
>>62219948
It's all relative, anon. You think they're happy not beating the market anymore? They've become a defensive investment option not by choice.

62219974
>>62219808
This is true, profitability does not matter in stock prices anymore. Their investment strategies only work today if they go for dividend stocks, otherwise in the current climate if you buy a stock that isn't memed enough, they can make a lot of money it won't get you anything, stock price will just stay low. Prices are not currently connected to the underlying companies

62222204
>>62219808
It's always like this before the music stops.

62224098
>>62219808
This is the biggest bullshit story in all of finance
>Buy and hold for 50 years through humanities greatest run up
>Genius
Fuck you

62224175
Basically the business model now is to offer an ‘S&P without the hype’ ETF, while also sitting on huge piles of cash waiting for a major crash. I don’t really see a tragedy in that.

62224182
>>62219808
its GEICO and a railroad. boohoo it didnt keep up with google, amazon, microsoft, nvda

62224186
>>62224175
>doesnt deploy the cash during covid or last april
there is your tragedy

62224198
>>62219974
>profitability does not matter in stock prices anymore
lol

item.title: Taxes Thread — 20 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Taxes Thread

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-13 02:05 UTC

OP

Can any USA tax lads please go more in depth and in detail about how to set up a tax efficient business+trust?

Particularly how to be the most tax efficient with a business that is also protected by within a trust? S-Corp or C-Corp? Both in the same state? Offshore? How do you avoid double taxation?

Most importantly, how would you invest personal and business income in long term stocks that you select?

How would you deal with income you got from an actual job somewhere which is not part of the your business but that you wanted protected by a trust and invested in your choice of stocks?

What would all the target tax rates be?

Please detail everything. Provide tips and cover all risks, etc. I have tried studying this on my own but the scenario I want in which all income from business and personal in custom selected stocks while protected by trust and taxed as small as possible, complicates things.

Early replies (first 3)

62218156
>>62218140
consult a great tax attorney and a cpa

62218211
>>62218140
if you make a personal s-corp you can expense everything in CA like paying for your own health insurance for example but you pay a minimum franchise tax of $800 even if you made nothing so not great for side-hustles owned by me that made nothing

62218357
>>62218140
Formula F1. If you can spare 10m, sponsor an F1 team and then expense your entire life

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62218371
>>62218140
Totally off-topic but it really makes me seethe that I’m really out here paying 25 percent of my income to pedophiles, then on top of that I get taxed on investments, purchasing anything, property, etc. Our ancestors tarred and feathered tax collectors over a 2 percent tax on stamps and now we work for three months out of the year for the government.

62218405
>>62218211
This is basically the grift. You set up an S-Corp and deduct everything as a “business expense.”

It is also illegal to claim a business deduction for personal expenses, but this never stopped black people on TikTok from expensing the lease payments on their Chryslers.

62218495
>>62218140
I hate redditors and normies and low iq's. Pic related is such a retarded analogy. The company gets tax deductions because those expenditures were related to income generation. A person can do the same thing by getting tax deductions on things deemed necessary for work or income generation. You spending your income on overpriced doordash is not related to income generation.

62218507
>>62218357
Yeah just pay 10m to save 4m in taxes goy. Not a very smart jew. This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway, or you needed the marketing regardless.

62218524
the business spent that 3.9B on salaries to all the retards making 60k

62218651
I paid like 95k in income tax then got clocked for 4k more when I did my taxes.

62218760
>>62218140
Unless you make serious cash, this is the best you can hope for.

62218844
>>62218495
huh? i can't work hungry

62218962
>>62218140
Talk to @TheJerzWay on X (he will require payment), but he's a pro. He'll probably also recommend getting your REPS and residency in Puerto Rico.

62219425
>>62218140
Just don't pay your taxes
Most of them go towards jews and the rest goes towards trying to bring in as many third world subhumans to replace you as possible
It is probably the most cucked thing you can do in the current age, especially in the U.S
Anyone who disagrees with this should respond with "I am a good goy cuck" because there's no rationalisation or argument to make what I'm saying any less true.

62219490
>>62218140
>That's not–
You don't pay taxes at all, do you?
You don't know what a Standard Deduction is, do you?
>File by May 15th
Yeah, whoever wrote this has never paid taxes in their life.
>>62218507
>This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway,
He was. They all do. This is "Hollywood accounting". But now he can use his business to fund it with no risk to his personal assets.

62219495
>>62218844
Your basic needs are covered by the standard deduction.

62220996
>>62218357
Good job shlomo!

62221525
>>62218140
Tell me you don't know the fucking difference between a W2 and a 1099 without telling me.

62223970
>>62218405
Ben affleck explains how to do this in the Accountant movie.

62224000
>>62218405
Yes, but ultimately this just lets you live an inflated lifestyle at a discount. If you don't really want a G wagon, even making it tax deductible doesn't really help because you've still got to pay for the non deductible amount. And if you don't have a business that lets you pretend like maybe you need it (ie. A real estate agent taking wealthy clients to see homes)

62224006
>>62218140
>60k income
>58k expenses
yeah no wonder the person who made this meme doesen't understand taxes

item.title: Labour shortage — 5 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Labour shortage

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:40 UTC

OP

How is there a labour shortage if you apply on indeed and there's 2000 people applying for the same job? For every 300 jobs you apply you get 1 interview and they act like you've giving an interview for the position of the CEO of a transnational mega corporation. Why are they so fucking picky if there's a labour shortage? I remember giving a interview to be a bus boy and I told the interviewer that I thought I'd find the job easy and he got angry and said it was very difficult job. I never got called back. I worked a dead end job for 2 years and somehow I got it while applying for another job initially. You had to be able to work between anytime from 5am to 1am and work 60 hours a week and almost everyone working there was an asylum seeker living in hotels who had come on student visas. They were all planning on either saving up enough money and then going back to their countries or planned on entering the US illegally and getting bank loans (free money) from american banks. I quit the job since it was going no where and I needed a better paying job. Now I'm looking for a job for the summer before I start my last year of college but I am reminded of how fucked the job market is.

Early replies (first 3)

62224241
>>62224230
I have 70k CAD. How can I third world max?

62224247
>>62224230
who told you there was a labor shortage

62224252
>>62224247
The government telling me the reason for why we have mass migration. Apparently it's due to a labour shortage.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224271
>>62224252
there's a labor shortage of farm workers, meat processors, etc. jobs that you won't do.

62224278
>>62224230
There is no shortage, the corporations just want to pay less for labor so they get the government to flood the market with turd worlders.
>>62224271
I'd pack meat for the right price, problem is they don't wanna pay that price. If I offered you the job of sucking my dick for $5 a day, and you refused, suddenly there's a labor shortage.

item.title: Chainlink: investors are stunned as the CL — 16 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink: investors are stunned as the CL

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-13 02:19 UTC

OP

team now claims a token can only be considered "sold" if a research study confirms the price goes down after.

Early replies (first 3)

62216860
can somebody confirm if this is correct?

i'm panicking now because i may have done my taxes wrong. i reported every trade where i sold something and not just the ones where the asset went down after.

i didnt realize you needed to keep tracking price afterwards...

62216869
yeah but im just not gonna sell

62216873
>>62216852
Interesting, I haven't been on this board in quite a while (5 or 6 months now??) and came by now with everything pumping just to see what's up and it's weird how there are STILL LINK fudders making posts all the time here to what are probably like 20 people left even on this board.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62216888
question - if it's not a sale, then what is it?

it appears that the tokens are sent from the foundation's treasury to a cex. do they just keep the coins there at that point? is that why it's not considered a sale?

62216897
>>62216869
>yeah but im just not gonna sell

i think you replied to wrong thread?

>>62216873
>everything pumping

is it? prices look the same as 5 years ago, if not worse for many coins

62216955
>>62216860
Anon, if you're moving your tokens from a "team wallet" what else are you planning to do with it? Move it into another reserve wallet? Come on. They're selling it.

62216980
>>62216888
That paper talks about 2023, where BTC rose almost throughout the whole year.
So LINK going up was almost entirely driven by BTC rising and dragging the rest of the market with it. Did Grayscale just completely miss this? Surely they couldn't be that incompetent, as it would have been almost impossible for LINK to not increase in price when BTC did somewhere between a 2.5x - 3x.

62216990
>>62216873
>>62216886
we are the few, the proud, the fuddies

62217098
>>62216980
>Did Grayscale just completely miss this?
Link is literally a product they're selling and making money off, they're incentivized to lie
look at the latest link token papers released
they're all completely detached from reality and parroting CLL talking points

62217160
>>62216852
>>62216860
none of this has anything to do with the screenshot
you are mentally ill

62217198
kek chainlink is such an obvious scam lmao

62220331
>>62216888
checked, when you zoom out you notice that the chainlink token unlocks are bullish

62221421
>>62216955
>Anon, if you're moving your tokens from a "team wallet" what else are you planning to do with it? Move it into another reserve wallet? Come on. They're selling it.


that was my conclusion as well but i just wanted a sanity check

it almost feels illegal they are telling people it's not a sale

62221433
>>62217160
>none of this has anything to do with the screenshot

it was a analogy where we are defining what a sale is.

>you are mentally ill

nah i'm good

62224041
>>62216852
Lol... but... price... chart???

item.title: >never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak… — 6 replies — 8.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:29 UTC

OP

>never had a gf
>never been on a date
>now 36
>had jobs
>go to gym
>finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25
>make multiple dating profiles on different apps
>zero matches

So I'm a confirmed subhuman. And, judging by the responses to ugly males who post their pics on Reddit and ask why they get no matches, normies openly laugh at me and think I deserve a low status life of slavery.

Why should I give a fuck about putting in effort at work or saving the west or whatever? Any sort of participation is a scam for someone like me.

Early replies (first 3)

62223666
>>62223664
>>>/adv/

62223769
>>62223664
depends how tall u are and if u have any real ugliness causing things like deformity or burn scars etc.

U probably are just average looking and if u arent 5'4'' u will find a gf

62224072
>>62223664
are you swiping on fat women or only the stacies?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224114
Plapjak won.

62224205
>>62223664
I doesn't have to be this way. You can get a passport and airplane ticket.

62224248
>>62223664
>36
>looked like shit
>expects matches on dating apps
What exactly have you been doing for the last 18 years of your adult life? At least do a trade or become a truck driver if (I assume) you didn't go to college. Are you seriously this fucking retarded?

item.title: INJ — 3 replies — 3.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
INJ

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:24 UTC

OP

10x easier

Early replies (first 3)

62220677
>looks at chart
ah looks like the classic pump n dump by the INJ team
no refunds!

62224218
>injective protocol

62224238
>>62220231
>shitcoins in 2026
How old are you buddy?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 18 replies — 33.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:40 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222823
>>62219625
my most successful strategies don't care about high winrate, they just win big on a few and cut losses early on the rest. so in that case, less trades = less chances to win big.

62222917
>>62219625
>Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right?
you'll actually get a smoother equity curve if you take more trades but lower risk-per-trade. if you trade less you'll have more luck-based variance in your equity curve.

on win-rate & RR: 50% win rate and 1:1RR really isnt good. its much easier to increase to 2:1RR than it is to try to control your win-rate over large amounts of trades.

i think your best path would be to choose the top 1-3 strategies you have and either increase your RR or try to get your win-rate towards 70% in a scalping style.

62223260
ive typed up like 5 responses but 4chan keeps saying its spam so im shortening my responses a LOT to see if it'll be let thru.

62222823
How do you apply that mechanically? When do you cut losses early? In a large sample, some trades go deep red and still win. Every time I’ve tested early cuts, winrate drops.

62222917
I wanted to filter bad trades using some pre-entry quality metric, but it doesn’t seem that simple.

I’m also not sure how taking more trades with lower risk smooths equity, since scaling up with prop firms brings the drawdown risk back anyway.

I agree the winrate and RR aren’t great, but it’s the best I’ve found. Most people claiming 2:1 RR don’t really hold full size to 2R. They trim early, or if they do hold, their winrate is often too low for prop firm rules.

It’s hard to increase RR without hurting winrate. Every TP or SL adjustment I’ve tested makes winrate suffer. All the lines perform similarly. They help fill drawdown gaps, but also create the risk of all losing on the same day.

I don’t need huge returns, just a smooth strategy with high enough winrate and decent RR to consistently reach payouts.

62223406
>>62223260
>I’m also not sure how taking more trades with lower risk smooths equity
i explained this in my post, you encounter more luck-based variance on the upside and downside with lower trade volume. if your 95% quartile losing streak is 10-15 trades in a row you'll be in a smaller drawdown if your average risk per trade is smaller.

as a general rule, your win-rate and RR exist on a sliding scale. on one end you win 20% but its 5:1 RR and the other end you win 90% but its 0.25:1 RR. basically pick your poison and make sure your trading is actually consistent (not taking trades outside your system).

im a professional futures trader (CTA) and my system averages 35% win rate but 3:1 RR. i've used the same style to pass prop firm evaluations in the past but like you've eluded to, its not the best deal and you are limited by their specific rules. sometimes i have 60%+ win-rate over 1-2 weeks and other weeks i dont get a single win. overall i make money for my clients and return between 80%-120% annualized.

62223681
>>62223406
I’d definitely prefer to lean toward the higher winrate system. With prop firms, blowing accounts is kind of expected. Even if a strategy backtests badly long term, as long as it has strong win streaks before the inevitable losses, I feel like I can engineer it to pass evals, reach a payout, and make money before the losses hit.

So I don’t necessarily need something that wins long term. I need something that wins consistently enough to get to a payout. That difference feels important enough that there should be something workable there, no?

Even with my current strategy, it’s theoretically profitable over time, but like the screenshot shows, it can still go through drawdowns of a few hundred to a few thousand points. Unless I have a huge cushion on the prop firm account, there’s a good chance I blow the account before the edge plays out.

That’s why I prefer a high winrate system. If I can reach a payout first, I don’t really care if the account eventually blows after that. If I’m spending $100–$200 on an account plus resets, but can pull out $1k–$2k, then that’s worth it to me.

How come you have clients? Why not just trade for yourself and keep on scaling up?

62223765
>>62223681
sounds like you want to be a scalper, nothing wrong with that but its limiting for maximizing profits. whats difficult with scalping is being glued to the screen & being paranoid of missing trades by seconds.

If you're only trading NQ or ES mostly on the long side, of course backtesting & optimizing will tell you to just hold through drawdown because it always returns to all time highs. this is a phenomenon that only occurs in US stocks and its teaching you bad habits for trading. a good trader always minimizes losses when they're wrong and seeks to maximize profits when they're right. especially for a 1:1 RR system, its devastating to realize losing trades that are 2+ risk units from your average because it takes so many wins to gain that 1 big loss back.

>How come you have clients? Why not just trade for yourself and keep on scaling up?
even though i have clients, i basically have complete freedom and discretion for my trading within a certain defined range of the 8 markets i trade and risk levels. I write the rules I want. Im doing this as a career & everyone in the finance industry knows that you get rich with other people's money. most of my networth is invested along side my clients so im just gaining greater scale this way. I can bring on client accounts with millions of dollars way faster than what I can earn risking my own capital, the scaling is on another level.

62223860
>>62223765
Depends on what you mean by scalper. The strategies I’m using usually hold positions anywhere from 30 minutes to a few hours, sometimes the whole market day. They’re not super short-term in-and-out trades.

Since they’re rule-based and mechanical, the ideal setup would be to automate entries and step away. I’m not a fan of discretionary trading. I don’t need the whole pie, or even a slice. I just want a few crumbs and make some money and retire early.

>backtesting & optimizing will tell you to just hold through drawdown because it always returns to all-time highs
That is a valid concern but it doesn't apply to my strategy to my knowledge. One way I tested my strategy was through random 30-day simulations and pure 30-day sims.
Example:
Pick a random date walk forward 30 days while trading each day.
Or pick a random day and trade it pick another random day and trade it repeat for 30 days.

In both cases, the strategy was profitable overall. Around 75% of the sims ended green. The picture shows those sims.

If I had deep pockets, this would be simple: just run the strategy live and let it work. If I had traded just 1 NQ contract per trade since its inception around 5 years ago, I’d be looking at nearly $1M in profit, with the worst drawdowns around $40k–$50k. The math goes that I'd need about 50-100K to start, which is no small amount.

The issue is I don’t have that kind of capital. If I trade it properly with my current funds, I’d basically be making pennies. That’s why I need to make it work through prop firms at least to start out.

Again, I ain't asking anyone for money. I am asking if anyone here has knowledge with statistics and math, or some quantitative background. the strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones.

62224145
bump. y'all make posts about wanting an easy way out but no one puts in the work. ive been trying to make something work for years at this point and i feel like im on the cusp of something, but i realize what im looking for is outside my knowledge. i aint smart enough for this. ive tried so many methods but im hitting dead ends. i need the help of someone who's smarter than me here

62224160
>>62224145
I'm working on the same thing but I have no interest in helping anyone, why would I do that?

62224188
>>62224160
Fair point. The way I see it, I don’t mind if you know the strategy, especially if you end up helping me. That’s the least I could do.

My goal isn’t to milk it forever. I just want to use it to reach a point where I can retire and live a simple, happy life. After that, whatever happens to the strategies or lines doesn’t really matter to me. If you wanted to keep using them and scale them further, I’d be fine with that.

You mentioned you were working on something similar. How far did you get with it? What kind of metrics or performance were you seeing?

62224200
>>62219625
post pseudo code

62224207
>>62224200
the strategies are a mixture of VWAP and ORB. i used a base idea and iterated from there, testing different rule sets and requirements before deciding on the current setup. do you have a background in statistics or quantitative analysis?

62224225
>>62223860
holding for seconds or minutes at a time is usually a scalper. if you're holding for 30 mins to hours+ i dont think 1:1RR is efficient, there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing and achieve the same reward.

clearly you have some type of edge. you could either trade your own capital in MNQ contracts or try to make the drawdowns fit for the prop firm plan. you dont have to trade full size NQs you could have a lot more flexibility with MNQ.

>strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones
this is where you have to drill down to every trade taken and qualitatively sort out the lower quality trades. theres certainly nuances and details that differ across the trades taken so finding a better way to filter out the bad ones should yield better efficiency. you cant do that type of work through top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

62224267
>>62224188
I have it working, it was running live for a few weeks. Made me an average 1% per day, but upon the first sign of danger I panicked and turned it off. Working on retooling it to notify me and I can configure it on the fly.

62224277
>>62224225

>there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing

Well anon, that’s basically the core problem. I don’t know how to qualitatively filter out lower-quality trades.

I’ve tried pulling data at the exact point of entry, things like EntryFromOpenPct, EntryFromVWAPPct, Prior10RangePct, Prior30ChurnPct, DayRangeSoFarPct, ES confirmation, EMA state, signal candle body/range, stop distance, target distance, planned RR, and a few other metrics.

The idea was to see if winners or losers had anything consistent in common, then build some kind of grading system to decide before entry whether a trade was worth taking. But that ended up being a dead end too. Although I am still testing variants of that as we speak with harsher filters, I'm not super hopeful.

I also don’t want to rely entirely on ChatGPT or AI to generate ideas, because it could easily lead me astray and I wouldn’t know, since I’m out of my element here. That’s why I’m stuck.

>you don’t have to trade full-size NQs, you could have more flexibility with MNQ

I looked into that too. The issue is that if I size down enough to survive the drawdowns, my scale becomes too small. It would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.

And once I run the math, I’d be spending 3–5 hours a day trading for basically nothing. Not even $100/day in actual profit after costs and resets.

So technically, I do have a path to payouts right now. The problem is the math says it’s just not worth it.

>top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

Sometimes if I have an idea, I’ll manually backtest it through a few days or weeks first. If it shows promise, then I’ll programmatically test it across the full dataset.

There are definitely some strategies that work well in specific regimes. The problem is that by the time you realize the regime has shifted, you’ve usually already given back most or all of what you made from it.

item.title: Nintendo Criminally Undervalued — 89 replies — 23.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Criminally Undervalued

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-05-13 00:24 UTC

OP

Honestly believe Nintendo is grossly undervalued. Considering a buy at current levels. Thing is, I’ve never bought an ADR before. What are the advantages/disadvantages of buying an ADR

Early replies (first 3)

62212154
>>62212149
Not buying that bing bing wahoo slop. Sorry.

62212158
>>62212149
I tried this when it dropped to 16. Stopped out at 12. I would let it bleed for a while might go lower.


About ADRS… beats buying on the tokyo exchange. Its 100 shares minimum on tokyo. Adr you can buy any amount. Only disadvantage is lower liquidity. If you want the actual shares use ibkr for tokyo, otherwise NTDOY is fine.

62212161
>>62212149
Nintendo was trading around 3 dollars during the wii u saga. It can easily drop another 50% from here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213657
>>62213569
I'd buy at the 5 range

62213925
Crashing 12%
LMFAO LOL

62213949
>below LINK
Ohhhhhh Nintendieeeeeeeeeees

62213977
>>62213235
>You are aware that shares can go to zero right?
>"I don't mind it going to zero"
Do you know how to read? More upside doesn't mean stock will bounce up and down. It means the reward ratio of it being 0 is close to impossible due to it being backed by Tencent, having enough equity to pay off the debt, having IP's 4 times worth more than the debt.

>>62213213
>Gamers are so fucking stupid it's unreal. Ubisoft is a dying company, 9/10 of their projects don't make enough money
Wow what thoughtful response. Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time. The entire company has restructured, they've gotten old game devs, quality controller much stricter. You can easily see it in AC BF Resynced and majority of the game was still made under the old team. Also, Tencent isn't going to let their 800m (and additional 1 billion on IP) dollar investment just go into flames. Ubisoft made a deal with the board (including Tencent) to not be takeover by Tencent for 8 years in 2022 to allow for a turnaround. This deadline is in 4 years. Tencent will want to fully take over the company by 2030 and have every incentive to not allow the debters to take their investment and IP.

62213982
>>62213925
Nintendos Market cap is below Activision when it got bought out for 69Billion. I doubt Nintendo isn't worth 55B.

62214016
Gaming is dead. All new games are something redone and done again, remakes and remasters, nostalgia bait and whatever. A lot of old IP are being killed by woke shit. A lot of people are just buying emulation consoles and playing old games. The last console generation with a huge amount of good games was ps2 (just for example: gta3, gta vc, gta sa). After that, the games that people are playing like crazy are the ones that people are addicted for decades like cs and dota.

62214017
>>62214016
Games that have online multiplayer with micro transactions, that's why on ps2 we had 3 gta, but gta v had 3 playstations.

62214026
>>62212149
Not buying your bing bing wahoo. Simple as

62214263
>>62212192
>ubislop
Im sorry to hear that

62214411
>>62212203
How do you think the stat of a gorillion % of stockpickers underperform arose.

62215019
>>62212149
bing bing wahoo slop top

62215031
https://www.zerohedge.com/markets/memory-crunch-sends-nintendo-shares-deeper-bear-market

I told you so and don't run away from your public shaming OP.

62215101
>>62214263
Pretty sure Guillemot has ties to the French government. It'll be ok.

62215398
>>62215031
You don’t base your investment decisions off zerohedge slop, do you?

62215752
>>62215398
I used to, and yea they lost me a lot of money the damn Bulgarian Russian fucks.

62215887
>>62212149
>undervalued
How, why? Explain this without analysing price but the actual valuation of the company.

62215912
>>62213519
Lmao I can feel the desperation thought your post.
Next time buy s&p500 fag.

62215923
>>62215912
>Through your post
Damn spell checker

62215940
>>62215887
Nintendo is the cultural pillar of japanese economy.

62215953
I don't fully get it.
Nintendo makes most of its money from software sales right? That side is hardly affected by hw issues.
They might lose profits on console sales and the worst thing being diminished install base growth which affects the game sales.
Have they really been that overvalued?
They are not going anywhere and their games will continue selling millions.

62216426
>>62213213
It's why we see sony getting into cars. When IMO they need to get into appliances since the margins are better.

62217444
>>62213320
Yes, but so far Nintendo is going full hollywood and theme parks, which isn't ideal. They should go harder on other shit for the stock to go higher, Switch 2 sales are already in a decline.
>>62213977
>Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time
>Last 4 games
Look at the list here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Ubisoft_games:_2020%E2%80%93present
I want you to look at the list here and dare if any of these are successful besides maybe AC Shadows, which isn't a smash hit as they would've wanted to get out of Tencent. If your only successful product line is from the AC series and nothing else, then you're looking at a company that's in the brink of ruin. Tencent will wait for Ubisoft to keep shooting itself in the foot until they get every worthwhile IP of theirs for cheap. I'm not saying this for you by the way, I'm saying this for everyone else not to be retarded and buy Ubisoft stocks.
>>62216426
Appliances are hard when they have stiff competition from Korea and China. Sony owns Music labels, animation studios and various many things but they still need to downsize to become more profitable. Their phones are non-existent, they're making joint operations with TCL on TVs now. If anything they need to look at Panasonic and just downsize and focus on extremely profitable industries and not bother with anything else.

62217766
>>62212192
>unironically Ubisoft
zamn

62219639
>>62217444
Kino reply. Got any more info on Sony?

62219641
Nintendo neglects their core business.
It's fine to really want to make money on licensing. But it's not fine to let the game's unit languish. They deserve to be beaten down.

62219725
Zoomers and Alphas aren`t buy trash like Milenials manchild.

Their games run better on android emulator than their own videogame.

62219799
>>62212612
Nintendo only owns a third of The Pokemon Company.

62221370
>>62219725
Good morning saar

62222931
>>62212192
the first half of your post is gold info
the 2nd half is poo poo

62223716
>>62217444
>I want you to look at the list here and dare if any of these are successful besides maybe AC Shadows

>The Division 2
10m Copies sold
>Crew 2
1.4M sold, above dev cost
>Assassin's Creed Valhalla
Best selling AC by far to date generating 1B in revenue (More than the current value of the company lol)
>Rainbow Six
Surpassed 100M registered players. Generatd 3.8B revenue since release.
30M active montly players.
>Far Cry 6
2m units sold on only steam. Game was profitable.
>Just Dance 2022S
Sold 4M globally
>Assassin's Creed Mirage
Total sales 5m with 5M on Ubisoft+
>Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora
Top selling Xbox games of 2024
>Prince of Persia: The Lost Crown
low cost metroidvania, sold like 1.3m
>Anno 117: Pax Romana
Was the fastest selling Anno series games.

I mean these are HUGE amounts of games that did well in only... 6 years?
>If your only successful product line is from the AC series and nothing else, then you're looking at a company that's in the brink of ruin.

That's flawed logic. The company needs to be continuing to go negative cashflow for it to be in the brink of ruin. Ubisoft had a few bad titles.

>Tencent will wait for Ubisoft to keep shooting itself in the foot
They already paid them half of the debt for 26% for 3 IP's with no control with buyback period. Ubisoft will do fine with the recontruction they're doing, calling old devs and new QC standards. It already shows in Black Flag. They have 45 Studios around the world with 2 billion in revenue. They can easily fire people and sell assets to pay off the debt.


>I'm not saying this for you by the way, I'm saying this for everyone else not to be retarded and buy Ubisoft stocks.
We'll see who'll have the laugh by July 9th. It's the most shorted stock in France atm, there will be a short squeeze to back to book value at least once we see the reviews, total sales and stuff. Screen Cap this.

item.title: V shaped dip edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the movies >Gam… — 35 replies — 22.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
V shaped dip edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the movies >Gam…

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-05-13 02:41 UTC

OP

V shaped dip edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/NyzbAsX4

Old >>62222475

Early replies (first 3)

62223155
So the GME baggies are getting fucked. Again lol

62223156
>NUGODS

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62223160
>>62223148
She was involved in creating CDOs and SIVs which were partly responsible for the 2008 mess.
Then moved to the metals desk at JP Morgan. She wasn't popular....

62223161
>>62223157
Anthropic up to 900B

62223162
Quick lads! We've got minutes!
Minutes I tells ya!

62223163
>>62223141
>no /smg/ in title
This is becoming a regular thing

62223164
>>62223155
do gme shareholders go to gme stores? i cant remember the last time i saw one.

62223166
>>62223147
I don't trust banks, they will rat you out. I just cash out into gold whenever I hit the threshold.

62223169
>>62223163
It's almost as if he does it on purpose.

62223171
>futures

62223172
>>62223162
Burry really is just the average smg bear

62223177
>>62223162
Must be doomsday clock minutes

62223178
>>62223172
burry was right once

62223179
>>62223166
I've never been audited despite 7 years of claiming benefits. The only thing they have is an automatic verifier that checks some employment, but not all.

My stock did not go up today
